log.why: Mapped from structured Casting Time Value.

fields.comment: Mapped from structured Casting Time Value. It maps to `castingTime.value`. It is populated from the structured markdown label `Casting Time Value`. Purpose: castingTime.value exists so canonical prose, structured markdown, and runtime JSON do not silently disagree about this mechanic. Populate it from the structured markdown field `Casting Time Value`, after that field has been checked against canonical prose. It maps to runtime JSON path `castingTime.value`. The value type is `number`. This is expected on every spell/template instance. Because this field is required, every spell should carry a deliberate value rather than relying on absence.

acceptedValueNotes.action: Use `action` for castingTime.unit only when the canonical spell prose supports that exact mechanic; otherwise use a more specific value or `not_applicable` if available.
acceptedValueNotes.bonus_action: Use `bonus_action` for castingTime.unit only when the canonical spell prose supports that exact mechanic; otherwise use a more specific value or `not_applicable` if available.
acceptedValueNotes.reaction: Use `reaction` for castingTime.unit only when the canonical spell prose supports that exact mechanic; otherwise use a more specific value or `not_applicable` if available.
acceptedValueNotes.minute: Use when castingTime.unit is measured in minutes; keep the numeric duration or casting amount in the paired value field.
acceptedValueNotes.hour: Use when castingTime.unit is measured in hours; keep the numeric duration or casting amount in the paired value field.
acceptedValueNotes.special: Allowed value for castingTime.unit; use only when the canonical spell text supports this exact mechanic.
